mechanism: Thioredoxin reductase inhibitor → increases ROS and induces apoptosis
additional_notes: Reported cytotoxicity in AML cell lines and primary AML samples; redox‑targeting agent; often tested in combinations.

mechanism: Forms complexes (with copper) that inhibit proteasome and aldehyde dehydrogenase; promotes ROS
additional_notes: Activity typically copper‑dependent; shown to kill AML cells and leukemia stem‑like cells in studies.

mechanism: Iron chelation and inhibition of ribonucleotide reductase; affects cell cycle
additional_notes: Shown anti‑leukemic activity in vitro and in vivo in some studies.

mechanism: Induces ER stress; inhibits AKT signaling and proteasome‑related pathways
additional_notes: Tested in AML models as apoptosis inducer and chemosensitizer.

mechanism: Disrupts tubulin and has multiple off‑target effects
additional_notes: Preclinical anticancer activity reported in hematologic malignancies including AML models.

mechanism: Mitochondrial/energetic disruption and modulation of autophagy pathways
additional_notes: Emerging preclinical evidence for antiproliferative activity in some cancers including blood cancers.

mechanism: Complex I inhibition → AMPK activation and metabolic stress
additional_notes: Many studies report antiproliferative effects and synergy with other agents in AML models.

mechanism: Inhibit HMG‑CoA reductase → affect prenylation and cell signaling
additional_notes: Examples listed: simvastatin, pravastatin; can induce apoptosis and sensitize AML cells to chemotherapy.

mechanism: Histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibition → epigenetic modulation
additional_notes: Evaluated in AML clinical trials, often combined with hypomethylating agents.

mechanism: Lysosomal/autophagy inhibition
additional_notes: Used in preclinical/clinical combinations to block protective autophagy in AML.

mechanism: Affects hedgehog/mTOR/angiogenesis pathways and some transporters
additional_notes: Repurposed in oncology with preclinical antiproliferative evidence.

mechanism: Inhibits BTK and some off‑target kinases
additional_notes: Approved for B‑cell malignancies; some AML subsets show BTK activity and preclinical growth inhibition or synergy.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK–STAT signaling pathway
additional_notes: May be active in AML with JAK/STAT activation or as combination partner.

mechanism: Inhibits COX‑2 and can have COX‑2‑independent pro‑apoptotic effects
additional_notes: Preclinical studies show reduced proliferation and enhanced apoptosis in some AML models.

mechanism: Alters lysosomal pH; can affect drug sequestration and autophagy
additional_notes: Listed as representative of PPIs that may sensitize cancer cells to chemotherapeutics.

mechanism: Disrupts ion transport; reported targeting of cancer stem‑like cells
additional_notes: Strong preclinical activity in some leukemia models but toxicity limits clinical translation in many countries.

mechanism: Form cytotoxic copper complexes (similar rationale to disulfiram)
additional_notes: Class rationale: agents that form cytotoxic copper complexes; specific compounds not listed in text.

mechanism: Modulates estrogen‑related growth pathways and has off‑target effects
additional_notes: Raloxifene also mentioned as an example; exploratory reports of antiproliferative activity in leukemia cell lines.

mechanism: Nuclear receptor modulation → differentiation induction in subsets of AML
additional_notes: Text refers to parenteral retinoids other than ATRA (e.g., bexarotene) as potential agents for some AML subgroups.

mechanism: Mitochondrial depolarization and metabolic collapse
additional_notes: Class rationale: AML cells (including LSCs) can be dependent on mitochondrial metabolism; text mentions older agents tested preclinically rather than specific approved drugs.
